questions.questionText:
Write these temperatures in order from coldest to warmest.
$$-8°C \qquad 3°C \qquad -1°C \qquad -15°C \qquad 0°C$$

markScheme.desc: All five in correct order (B1 for four correct)

questions.explanation: More negative = colder. Place on a number line.

questions.questionText:
Write the correct symbol $<$, $>$ or $=$ between each pair.
(a) $-3 \quad \square \quad -7$
(b) $0.4 \quad \square \quad \dfrac{2}{5}$
(c) $(-2)^2 \quad \square \quad -4$
workingSteps: (a) −3 is to the right of −7 on number line, so −3 > −7
questions.explanation: On a number line, numbers further right are larger.

questions.questionText: Write down all integers $n$ such that $-2 < n \leq 3$.
workingSteps: −2 < n means n is strictly greater than −2, so −2 is NOT included
n ≤ 3 means n can equal 3, so 3 IS included
questions.explanation: < means strictly greater; ≤ means 'less than or equal to'.
